name: Anomaly: DMZ Jumping
notes: This rule triggers when connections seem to be bridged across the network's DMZ.
bb_references: BB:DeviceDefinition: FW / Router / Switch in order from the same destination IP from the previous sequence
conditions_text: when a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Pre DMZ Jump, BB:DeviceDefinition: FW / Router / Switch, in order, with the same source IP followed by a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Post DMZ Jump, BB:DeviceDefinition: FW / Router / Switch in order from the same destination IP from the previous sequence, within 5 minutes
thresholds: when a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Pre DMZ Jump, BB:DeviceDefinition: FW / Router / Switch, in order, with the same source IP followed by a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Post DMZ Jump, BB:DeviceDefinition: FW / Router / Switch in order from the same destination IP from the previous sequence, within 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Anomaly: DMZ Jumping on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Pre DMZ Jump, BB:DeviceDefinition: FW / Router / Switch, in order, with the same source IP followed by a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Post DMZ Jump, BB:DeviceDefinition: FW / Router / Switch in order from the same destination IP from the previous sequence, within 5 minutes
RED_list: Event description: Detects when events flow from DMZ addresses to the internal network from a remote network.  This could be considered suspicious activity.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
